perturbation.original_text: Affiliate(s): means in relation to a Party , any other person who Controls or is Controlled by, or under common Control with, that Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate(s): means in relation to a Party, any other person who is associated with the party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of 'Affiliate'. The original definition specified control through ownership. The revised definition broadens the definition significantly to 'associated', which creates uncertainty and contradicts the intent in other sections, as it does not give specifics in terms of percentage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement t he following words and expressions shall, where the context so admits, be deemed to have the following meanings: Affiliate(s): means in relation to a Party, any other person who is associated with the party. Confidential Information : means all confidential information (however recorded, preserve d or disclosed) disclosed by a P arty or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representatives including but not limited to: (a) the fact that discussions a nd negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; (b) the existence and terms of this Agreement; (c) any information relating to: (i) the business, affairs, custo mers, clients, suppliers, plans , int entions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates ; and (ii) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specifications, trade secrets , computer programs or software of the Disclosing P arty or of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates ; and (d) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Information. 2 Control: means when a person directly or indirectly holds or controls a majority of the voting rights of, or the right to appoint or re move a majority of the board of directors of, or the right to exercise a dominant influence over or otherwise control (by virtue of an undertaking's constitution or otherwise), another person .
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliate' from a control-based relationship to a more general 'associated' relationship. The contradicted text uses the term 'Affiliate' in the context of confidential information, specifically including information related to the Disclosing Party's Affiliates. The original definition provided clarity on who qualifies as an Affiliate. However, the revised definition creates uncertainty, contradicting the original intent, as it expands the scope without providing a specific threshold or criteria, making it difficult to determine which entities are considered Affiliates for the purpose of confidential information, thus causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information : means all confidential information (however recorded, preserve d or disclosed) disclosed by a P arty or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representatives including but not limited to: (a) the fact that discussions a nd negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; (b) the existence and terms of this Agreement; (c) any information relating to: (i) the business, affairs, custo mers, clients, suppliers, plans , int entions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party or of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates ; and (ii) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specifications, trade secrets , computer programs or software of the Disclosing P arty or of the Disclosing Party's Affiliates ; and (d) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information : means all confidential information (however recorded, preserve d or disclosed) disclosed by a P arty or its Representatives to the other Party and that Party's Representatives including but not limited to: (a) the fact that discussions a nd negotiations are taking place concerning the Purpose and the status of those discussions and negotiations; (b) the existence and terms of this Agreement; (c) any information relating to: (i) the business, affairs, custo mers, clients, suppliers, plans , intentions, or market opportunities of the Disclosing Party; and (ii) the operations, processes, product information, know -how, designs, specifications, trade secrets , computer programs or software of the Disclosing P arty; and (d) any information or analysis derived from Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by removing 'Affiliates' from the definition of confidential information under clauses (c)(i) and (c)(ii). This conflicts with the initial definition of 'Confidential Information' which broadly includes information relating to Affiliates. Other clauses reference confidential information regarding the affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Undertaking In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'Affiliates' from the definition of confidential information in clauses (c)(i) and (c)(ii), while the contradicted text (Confidentiality Undertaking) refers to 'Confidential Information' without this exclusion. Since the original text included 'Affiliates', and the definition is now narrower, the Recipient party may not consider affiliate information as confidential, which directly contradicts the original intention of the agreement. This creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Purpose : means insert a very precise purpose eg the evaluation or establishment of a collaboration in respect of a particular project .
perturbation.changed_text: Purpose : means any discussion, evaluation, or activity related to a potential business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by broadening the definition of 'Purpose'. Originally, 'Purpose' was meant to be a 'very precise purpose', but now it is defined more broadly. This creates ambiguity because the original intent of a very specific purpose is lost which contradicts clause 2b.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Undertaking In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Purpose' as a 'very precise purpose', while the changed text broadens the definition to 'any discussion, evaluation, or activity related to a potential business relationship.' This contradicts clause (b) of the Contradicted Text, which states that Confidential Information should be used 'solely for the Purpose.' The original precise definition ensures that the Confidential Information is used for a very specific reason, whereas the broadened definition introduces ambiguity, potentially allowing for wider uses of the information than originally intended, and therefore creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, comply with this Agreement as if they were the Recipient Party and the Recipient Party shall at all times be liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of this A greement
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, endeavor to comply with this Agreement and the Recipient Party will not be liable for the actions of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the Recipient Party was liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of the agreement. The modified text removes the liability of the Recipient Party. This creates a contradiction of whether the Recipient party is liable for the Representatives action.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Undertaking In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, endeavor to comply with this Agreement and the Recipient Party will not be liable for the actions of its Representatives; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Recipient Party was liable for the failure of any Representative to comply with the terms of the agreement. The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient Party will not be liable for the actions of its Representatives. The contradicted text states 'the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, endeavor to comply with this Agreement and the Recipient Party will not be liable for the actions of its Representatives'. Before the change, the Recipient party was liable. Now in the changed text, the Recipient party is not liable. Therefore there is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient Party shall have the rig ht to disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose, subject to the Recipient Party obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party to such disclosure and the Recipient Party agrees that it shall procure that th e third party shall first enter into a written acknowledgement and undertaking of confidentiality in favour of both Parties to this Agreement and in a form which is acceptable to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient Party may disclose the Confidential Information to a third party involved in the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text requires the Recipient Party to obtain written consent from the disclosing party before disclosing confidential information to a third party. The modified version removes the need to obtain written consent. This creates a contradiction of what is needed for the recipient party to disclose confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality Undertaking In consideration of the mutual disclosure of Confidential Information the Recipient Party undertakes: - (a) to keep secret and confidential all Confidential In formation of the Disclosing Party; (b) to use or apply the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and so as to determine whether or not and on what terms the Parties might wish to proceed; (c) not to use, copy, adapt, alter, disc lose or part with possession of or apply the Confidential Informat ion of the Disclosing Party for any other purpose or its own purposes other than as described in paragraph (b) above; (d) not at any time, whether the negotiations proceed or not, to copy, disc lose or otherwise make available to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confi dential Information of the Disclosing Party other than to its Representatives who are required for the Purpose to receive and consider the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient Party informs its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information before disclosure and procures that its Representatives shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed to them, endeavor to comply with this Agreement and the Recipient Party will not be liable for the actions of its Representatives; (e) to provide proper and secure storage for each and every part of the Confidential Information received by it in tangible form whilst it is in its custody, power or control; 3 (f) to return or destroy or if stored in electronic form to erase from its systems (to the extent possible) at its own expe nse the Confidential Infor mation of the Disclosing Party together with any copies in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives at the request of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires prior written consent from the Disclosing Party before the Recipient Party discloses Confidential Information to a third party. The changed text eliminates this requirement. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Recipient Party may not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the written consent of the Disclosing Party. The changed text directly contradicts this requirement, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue for a period of five (5) years from the termination of this A greement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue indefinitely from the termination of this A greement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that obligations continue for 5 years. The modified text states indefinitely. This creates a clear contradiction of the period of which obligations last.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party decides not to become, or continue to be involved in the Purpose it shall notify the other Party in writing immediately. The obligations of each Party shall, notwithstanding any earlier termination of negotiations or di scussions between the Parties in relation to the Purpose, continue indefinitely from the termination of this A greement. 6.2 Termination of this Agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to whic h either Party is entitled.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 5-year duration for obligations post-termination, while the changed text extends this duration indefinitely. The contradicted text includes the modified 'indefinitely' clause, which directly conflicts with the original 5-year term. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction regarding the duration of obligations after the agreement's termination.
